name: Amphion
tagline: Open-source toolkit for audio, music, and speech generation
seoTitle: Amphion: Open-Source Audio & Speech Generation Toolkit
metaDescription: Amphion is an open-source toolkit for audio, music, and speech generation. It supports TTS, voice conversion, singing synthesis, text-to-audio, vocoders, and evaluation.
overview: Amphion is an open-source toolkit for generating audio, music, and speech. It pulls many separate generation tasks into one codebase so researchers and engineers can study and reproduce them in a single place.

The project covers a wide range of jobs: text-to-speech, singing voice synthesis, voice conversion, accent conversion, singing voice conversion, and text-to-audio. It also ships several neural vocoders and a set of objective evaluation metrics. Amphion adds interactive visualizations of classic models, which help newcomers see how the architectures work inside.
features: Text-to-speech with state-of-the-art architectures including FastSpeech2, VITS, VALL-E, NaturalSpeech2, and the non-autoregressive MaskGCT model
Voice conversion, accent conversion, and singing voice conversion, including zero-shot frameworks such as Vevo and FACodec
Text-to-audio generation built on a latent diffusion model in the style of AudioLDM and AUDIT
A collection of neural vocoders spanning GAN-based (HiFi-GAN, BigVGAN), flow-based, diffusion-based, and auto-regressive designs
Comprehensive objective evaluation metrics covering F0, energy, intelligibility, spectrogram distortion, and speaker similarity
Unified data preprocessing for common datasets plus support for the large-scale Emilia speech dataset and its Emilia-Pipe pipeline

gettingStarted.intro: Amphion can be installed with the setup installer using conda, or by pulling its Docker image. After installing, follow the task recipes in the repository to run a specific generation job.

steps.heading: Clone the repository
steps.body: Download the source code from GitHub and move into the project folder.

steps.heading: Create the Python environment
steps.body: Use conda to create and activate a dedicated environment for Amphion.
steps.code:
conda create --name amphion python=3.9.15
conda activate amphion

steps.heading: Install the dependencies
steps.body: Run the provided shell script to install the required Python packages.

steps.heading: Or use the Docker image
steps.body: If you prefer Docker, pull the official image and run it with GPU access, mounting your working directory.
steps.code:
docker pull realamphion/amphion
docker run --runtime=nvidia --gpus all -it -v .:/app realamphion/amphion

useCases: Reproducing and comparing speech and audio generation models for research and benchmarking
Building text-to-speech, voice conversion, or singing voice systems from a single shared codebase
Generating audio from text descriptions using diffusion-based text-to-audio models
Evaluating generated audio with consistent objective metrics for intelligibility, distortion, and speaker similarity
Teaching and learning how classic audio models work through Amphion's interactive visualizations
